item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the feedback and upvote. I'll see if I can adjust the dialogue for that part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you should provide an explanation for the d-class just finding a wrench behind a toilet.

Also,

Researcher ████: Alright, that's really weird.

seems too casual and, in my opinion, isn't very clinical tone-esque. Specifically the use of "really" and "weird". Not so much for "weird", however. Other than that, +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Come for the pun-tastic title, stay for the ungodly demented horror.

Those poor D-Class guys. Really.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you very much, glad you enjoyed the read.

Yes, the split personality thing is a bit ambiguous in the article itself. I left out some of the details from the original version, so the reader can interpret it as a possession as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought everything in the spoiler was laid out quite nice throughout the article, although I didn't get the split personality thing. Thought maybe the kid got possessed and was somehow fighting back. Good scip. +1 for the well laid out spoilers in comments with explanations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm wondering if I should put something in the article that was in the original. I left it out because I was afraid it would give away too much. Basically, at the end, the head researcher is supposed to say something like.

You know what the readings indicated. With a hume level of [REDACTED]1, half the population of █████ could wake up and find themselves inside D-10221's guts. It could happen; this thing is unstable. As is, it is contained within SCP-XXXX, probably of its own doing. Our job is to make sure it keeps itself contained.

-Head Researcher ██████.

It's redacted because a reading that low is atypical, and indicates an unstable area of reality. They want to understand it better before releasing the information for general viewing. Also, they don't want a GOI such as the Chaos Insurgency knowing about it, should an information breach occur.

If it helps the reader understand that the entity is a reality bender that can't control his effect on his surroundings, I'll add it.

Footnotes
1. Maybe .15-.2?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The article could be improved upon in terms of conveying the story, yes. It was originally much longer, where there were investigations, and an interview with a child psychologist, but I felt that it didn't quite fit in, because it was too long.

In the end, I took it out, and simplified it. True, the leeches and the corpse are simply red herrings intended to give the article a 'haunted house' feel (the corpse is one of the family members), with no real contribution to the story. Ultimately they were just byproducts of what I intended to be the main story.

The note is very ambiguous and vague, but it's that and the fluctuating hume readings that suggest it's a kid with some form of disorder and unconscious reality altering effects.

Sorry if I couldn't convey it well enough.

I'll add an explanation of what the article actually meant, as well as the clues:

+ explanation
- hide

Bedroom: The kid created this spacial anomaly to trap himself in, out of guilt, after he caused his parents to vanish.

Corpse: It's one of his family members.

Hume readings: The indicator that they're dealing with a reality bender, causing various anomalies in what is perceived as reality. Unlike other reality benders, his fluctuate at random intervals, which cause the electrical appliances and temperature to drop randomly, as well. This indicates that he does not have complete control over his abilities.

The [REDACTED] part implies that reality is severely bent out of proportion, to the point where it is uncommon for reality benders. That explains why the second D-Class found himself inside the first's twisted guts that merged with the piping, and existed in a "pocket dimension" where the researchers couldn't find him.

Note: It could have been done better, yes, but the original story was that the kid is suffering from a split personality disorder. His "bad" side has greater control over the reality affecting ability, but his "good" side doesn't because it's constantly fighting to keep the bad side under control. The note in this version is simplified, because originally, I just laid everything bare, and I wasn't sure if that was too direct. The note is written in the kid's handwriting to confirm to the reader that he's the only one alive in the house, thus, the one bending reality.

Disappearances: The rest of the imagery serves to paint a picture of what could happen when reality bending goes wrong, or if the bender has little to no control over his ability to alter reality.

Hope that helps to clarify what the clues were.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bah, you probably could have done a better job painting the story throughout the article instead of yet another note-at-the-end cop-out.

None of what you stated in the spoiler came through in the article, some of the imagery is nice, but there seems to be a huge disconnect between what you wanted to convey, and what you ended up writing.

Nothing - not the leeches, not the corpse, not the digestive fluids or the temporal shenanigans comes together in a way that makes it greater than the sum of its (admittedly creepy) parts, and it comes across as throwing scary shit at the wall to see what sticks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, glad you enjoyed the read

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jeepers! what did i just read!? that was scary! i liked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you very much, glad you enjoyed the read.

Welcome to the site!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good SCP! I like all the detail and the formal way of writing. I've only recently created my account but have read many SCPs before this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you very much, glad you enjoyed the read.

Believe it or not, my co-writer and I planned to write that dialogue years back, but didn't have a solid idea of where to implement it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a sucker for places where the spacetime continuum dun got all cattywampus, plus the dialogue has a certain banter to it I like.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Weird type article, with a 'haunted house' feeling, even though it isn't. It was written essentially to give the D-Class a hard time. Explanation is given below, but clues are available in the article itself.

+ spoiler
- hide

The child, SCP-3607-A, has reality altering capabilities. It manifested itself very recently, prior to the events of the article, and resulted in the disappearance of his family, when he got upset. He doesn't have full control over it, and he thinks there's something wrong with him, such as a split personality disorder.

He blamed himself and sealed himself inside the house.

Hope you like it.

Edit: Here's a more detailed explanation as to what the clues were,

+ explanation
- Title for hiding text

Bedroom: The kid created this spacial anomaly to trap himself in, out of guilt, after he caused his parents to vanish.

Corpse: It's one of his family members.

Fluctuating Hume readings: The indicator that they're dealing with a reality bender, causing various anomalies in what is perceived as reality. Unlike other reality benders, his fluctuate at random intervals, which cause the electrical appliances and temperature to drop randomly, as well. This indicates that he does not have complete control over his abilities.

The [REDACTED] part implies that reality is severely bent out of proportion, to the point where it is uncommon for reality benders. That explains why the second D-Class found himself inside the first's twisted guts that merged with the piping, and existed in a "pocket dimension" where the researchers couldn't find him.

The Note: It could have been done better, yes, but the original story was that the kid is suffering from a split personality disorder. His "bad" side has greater control over the reality affecting ability, but his "good" side doesn't because it's constantly fighting to keep the bad side under control. The note in this version is simplified, because originally, I just laid everything bare, and I wasn't sure if that was too direct. The note is written in the kid's handwriting to confirm to the reader that he's the only one alive in the house, thus, the one bending reality.

Disappearances: The rest of the imagery serves to paint a picture of what could happen when reality bending goes wrong, or if the bender has little to no control over his ability to alter reality.

Hope that helps to clarify what the clues were.
